reasoning: A 1% increase from a baseline Elo of ~2473 requires gaining nearly 25 points. In modern chess at the GM/IM level, gaining 25 points in less than one month (for the Nov 2025 resolution dates) is extremely difficult, as it requires an extraordinarily high performance rating over a large number of games. FIDE ratings are updated monthly. The base rate for this kind of short-term 1% increase is confirmed to be 0.007 (0.7%). Since the lower bound allowed by the rules is 0.02, I am assigning the minimum probability to the earliest date, slightly higher for the next, and moderately higher for the January 2026 date to account for the longer time horizon (about 3 months) allowing for more games to be played.
